title: Anomaly Detection
tagline: Detect anomalies in sensor and transaction data

sections.problem: Operations needed alerts when sensor or transaction metrics deviated from normal. Labels were scarce, so a supervised approach wasn't feasible for all anomaly types.
sections.data: Synthetic multivariate time series (e.g. 5 sensors) with injected anomalies: spikes, level shifts, and rare combinations. I used a rolling window to build feature vectors (mean, std, min, max over 1h windows).
sections.approach: I tried Isolation Forest and a simple autoencoder on standardized features. Threshold for 'anomaly' was set by the 99th percentile of anomaly scores on a clean holdout. Evaluation: precision and recall on an annotated test set (synthetic labels).
sections.results: Isolation Forest reached precision 0.89 and recall 0.76 at the chosen threshold. The score distribution below shows separation between normal and anomalous points.
next: Add simple rules for known failure modes.
Retrain or adapt threshold as distribution shifts.
